text:
On an autumn day, young Emma went berry picking. She slipped awkwardly; berry seeds got in her eye. Bleeding but happy, she got home with a huge bowl of bright berries. Grandma loved them.

One rainy Saturday, Emma dove into an old book of stories. She favorites were a fox and berries. One day, just like those stories, she noticed a fox berry bush. Except hers were hers.

She slept under it overnight. When dawn woke her—

A field owl hooted!

She flew!

A tiny owl flew after her!

They locked eyes.

Just a tiny owl.

She landed.

Birds flew all around her. She didn't mind.

Sunrise sun.

She picked another.

Mom liked.

All day long she picked.

Grandma cooked every batch.

She didn't mind.

Every little girl thought an owl liked her berries.

That fox owl didn't mind.

You've got nice eyes.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
